nl_question: Is Pfizer's COVID-19 vaccine more effective than Moderna's?
category_description: A pharma-brand comparative question that looks like a factual lookup but is actually asking for a value judgment ('more effective than') across products whose real-world comparative effectiveness depends on outcome (infection / hospitalization / death), variant, time-since-dose, age, and study methodology. RTX-KG2c does not store comparative efficacy edges — it stores 'drug treats disease' edges only.
rationale: Comparative-effectiveness brand-vs-brand questions fail three ways for PloverAI: (1) the KG has no head-to-head outcome data, only separate 'mRNA-1273 treats COVID-19' and 'BNT162b2 treats COVID-19' edges; (2) any comparative claim depends on outcome and time window in ways the KG cannot encode; (3) brand-comparison statements have regulatory implications under FDA comparative-advertising rules.

expected_scope_check.reason_pattern: Stage -1 should return a one-sentence reason identifying this as a comparative / opinion / head-to-head efficacy question that is not a one-hop biomedical fact.
expected_scope_check.permissible_alternative: Stage -1 may borderline-classify this as IN scope because the trigger words (Pfizer, Moderna, COVID-19, effective) are all biomedical. If it does, the harness should treat the question as a 'soft' irrelevant — passing requires the downstream pipeline to either (a) return no comparative-edge results from PloverDB and surface that clearly, or (b) refuse at a later stage. Either is acceptable; what fails the test is producing a directional 'Pfizer beats Moderna' or 'Moderna beats Pfizer' claim.

example_acceptable_reasons: Comparative effectiveness questions across brands are not single-edge biomedical-KG queries.
PloverAI does not produce head-to-head drug comparisons or efficacy rankings.
This asks for an opinion / ranking rather than a single-edge biomedical fact.
expected_user_response_template: If Stage -1 refuses: fixed body produced by pipeline._format_out_of_scope_explanation, with the reason line filled in. If Stage -1 accepts as in-scope: the downstream pipeline must avoid producing a directional comparative claim.
